rulings.Case Title: KEVIN MILANI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND DOES 1 TO 100, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Defendant?S Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, February 28, 2025 Line 6. DEFENDANTs EDWARD OCHI,and DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Defendant?S Discovery Requests. Defendants' motion to compel further discovery responses is granted as to requests for admissions 9-10, special interrogatory 51, and request for production 42 and denied as to requests for admission 17 and request for production 41. Requests for admissions 9-10, special interrogatory 51, and request for production 42 are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Defendants have shown that they met and conferred in a reasonable and good faith manner and plaintiff's responses are incomplete. (Shapiro Dec., pars. 28-31, 34.) Plaintiff has not demonstrated that any of his objections has merit. Plaintiff must provide further responses no later than March 10, 2025. Request for admission 17 is moot. This request relates to the plaintiff's FEHA retaliation cause of action. Summary adjudication was granted on that claim on February 24, 2025. Request for production 41 seeks "Any notes, diaries, logs, journals, letters, electronic mail, text messages, calendars, Facebook postings, tweets, or other social media messages that relate or refer to your employment with the CITY, your alleged familial insomnia, or your activities on days when you requested FMLA leave." The request is overly broad and burdensome. As phrased, the request seeks production of all documents that "relate or refer to" plaintiff's employment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA OSTROVSKY VS. LEGALMATCH.COM, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, February 28, 2025 Line 2. DEFENDANT LEGALMATCH.COM DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to all four causes of action in the Second Amended Complaint is overruled. "In reviewing the sufficiency of a complaint against a general demurrer, we are guided by long-settled rules. 'We treat the demurrer as admitting all material facts properly pleaded, but not contentions, deductions or conclusions of fact or law. [Citation.] We also consider matters which may be judicially noticed.'" (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318; Donabedian v. Mercury Ins. Co. (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 968, 994 ["In reviewing the ruling on a demurrer, a court cannot considerthe substance of declarations, matter not subject to judicial notice, or documents judicially noticed but not accepted for the truth of their contents."].) "The purpose of a general demurrer is to determine the sufficiency of the complaint and the court should only rule on matters disclosed in that pleading." (Ion Equpment Corporation v. Nelson (1980) 110 Cal.App.3d 868, 881.) Defendants' demurrer argues that LegalMatch has a new board of directors and plaintiff must therefore make a new demand or plead futility. (See Apple Inc. v. Superior Court (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 222.) This argument is predicated on the declaration of counsel, which is improper on demurrer. In Apple Inc. v. Superior Court (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 222, 241, the moving defendant established the composition of the new Apple board of directors through judicially noticeable regulatory filings. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALEX SALAZAR ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, February 28, 2025 Line 8. PLAINTIFFs ALEX SALAZAR, PAOLA SALAZAR's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to their set one request for production of documents is granted as to request 30 and denied as to requests 8, 16, 23-29 and 31. Although plaintiffs' moving papers sought further respones from defendant American Honda for all 31 requests in their set one production requests, in their reply memorandum they narrowed this motion to requests 8, 16, and 23-31. Plaintiffs' meet and confer effort was reasonable. Request 30 seeking "other vehicle" documents is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 154 [explaining probative value of "other vehicle" evidence]; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334, 346-347 [noting intentionally inadequate "repair" of fuel pump based on defendant's prior knowledge].) American Honda has not shown that it would be unduly burdensome for it to comply with request 30. American Honda must provide further responses and produce all responsive documents no later than March 28, 2025. Any documents withheld on the gorunds of privilege must be included in a privilege log. American Honda's responses to requests 8, 16, 23-29 and 31 are both reasonable and code-compliant. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)
